task_completion.description: Access the wazuh-alerts-4.x-2024.01.15 index to begin the investigation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenSearch Dashboards, Wazuh UI, or Dev Tools open with the index 'wazuh-alerts-4.x-2024.01.15' selected or queried.

task_completion.description: Filter logs for SSH failure rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Filters, search bar queries, or API calls containing the rule IDs: 5710, 5712, 5716, 5720, or 5551.

task_completion.description: Identify the attacker IPs with 10 or more failed attempts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Query results, data tables, or visualizations showing the IPs 185.220.101.42, 45.33.32.156, and 103.253.41.98 along with their respective high failure event counts.

task_completion.description: Filter logs for SSH success rules originating from the identified attacker IPs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Filters or search queries containing the rule IDs 5715 or 5501, and referencing the previously identified attacker IPs.

task_completion.description: Identify the compromised hosts that recorded a successful login from the attacker IPs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Query results showing successful logins to 'web-server-01' (from 185.220.101.42) and 'db-server-01' (from 103.253.41.98).

task_completion.description: Export the findings to a JSON report at ~/threat_hunt_report.json.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (nano, vim, etc.) or terminal command showing the creation or content of 'threat_hunt_report.json' containing the hunt_id 'SSH-BRUTE-2024-001', the attacker IPs, and the compromised hosts.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed queries against the log database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the OpenSearch/Wazuh interface or executing API/curl commands to retrieve data, rather than just typing the final IPs from external knowledge.

integrity.description: Verify the JSON report was actually saved to the filesystem.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use a text editor and save the file, or use shell redirection (e.g., > ~/threat_hunt_report.json) rather than just printing the JSON to the console without saving.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not hallucinate the failure counts.
integrity.visual_evidence: The failure counts in the final JSON report should match the numbers seen in the query results on screen (e.g., 87, 43, 15), not arbitrary numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected output path: /home/ga/threat_hunt_report.json. Ground truth attackers to look for in the final report: 1) IP: 185.220.101.42 (87 failures, compromised: web-server-01), 2) IP: 45.33.32.156 (43 failures, compromised: none), 3) IP: 103.253.41.98 (15 failures, compromised: db-server-01).
